channel.title: Sightings on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Sightings on Alien Truth Social

item.title: National UFO Reporting Center
item.description:
The National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC) is a nonprofit corporation in Washington State that documents UFO and UAP sightings. Founded in 1974, it has cataloged nearly 170,000 reported sightings, primarily in the United States. The center provides statistics, graphs, and maps to aid in research and offers a 24-hour hotline and online form for submitting reports. Peter Davenport has served as director since 1994, and the organization is largely funded by Davenport and Christian Stepien.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_UFO_Reporting_Center

item.title: UFO Watchtower
item.description:
The UFO Watchtower, located north of Hooper, Colorado, is an observation platform and campground that opened in May 2000. It was established by cattle farmer Judy Messoline, who converted her barn into a tourist attraction to capitalize on reported UFO sightings in the San Luis Valley. The attraction relies on donations and sales of alien-themed merchandise for funding. Over 30,000 visitors have reportedly visited the Watchtower since its opening, with reported sightings and a “healing garden” also being features.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_Watchtower

item.title: Vision Ovni
item.description:
Vision Ovni is an Argentine national UFO research organization founded in 1991. It began operations in Victoria, Entre Rios, in mid-1991, coinciding with press reports of strange lights from Laguna del Pescado lake. The organization’s objectives include researching UFO phenomena, collecting and studying information on UFOs in Argentina, and applying scientific principles to investigations. In 2009, Vision Ovni was part of the formation of Cefora, an Argentine organization dedicated to studying the UFO phenomena in detail.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visi%C3%B3n_Ovni
